description: Confounder: The model assumes weight_3d has shape (num_experts, hidden_size, intermediate_di

_meta.explanation: The rule 'SwiGLUExperts.reset_parameters initializes gate_proj and up_proj weights with std=1/sqrt(hidden_size), not using kaiming_uniform_'s default fan_in computation, to avoid incorrect scaling when weights are 3-D tensors.' does NOT hold here. The model assumes weight_3d has shape (num_experts, hidden_size, intermediate_dim) as in the rule, but here the last two dims are swapped, so using gate_std = 1/sqrt(hidden_size) no longer matches the actual fan-in dimension.
_meta.curriculum_note: Rule 'SwiGLUExperts.reset_parameters initializes gate_proj and up_proj weights with std=1/sqrt(hidden_size), not using kaiming_uniform_'s default fan_in computation, to avoid incorrect scaling when weights are 3-D tensors.' holds in the obvious case but breaks here. Distractor B is the hard negative — the rule naively applied.
